title: sapotaceae
intro: The sapotaceae rank, also known as the Sapotaceae family, is a group of flowering plants that includes approximately 150 species of trees and shrubs. This family is native to tropical regions of the world, particularly in Central and South America, Africa, and Southeast Asia. The Sapotaceae family is characterized by its large, fleshy fruits that are often edible, and its members are commonly known as sapotes. Members of this family are often used for their timber, and some species are cultivated for their fruit, which is eaten raw or used in the production of jams and preserves.
